# Proposed Solution

### Objectives

**Primary Objective:**  
To develop and pilot an open-source, offline-first R education toolkit that enables beginners in low-resource environments to learn R programming effectively.

**Specific Objectives:**
- Develop a structured beginner-friendly R curriculum (6–8 modules) in English and Hausa  
- Create an offline-accessible learning package  
- Design a train-the-trainer framework  
- Pilot the toolkit in Kano  
- Publish all materials as open-source  

---

### Methodology

The project will be implemented over approximately 3–4 months in five phases:

**Phase 1: Needs Assessment and Curriculum Design (Weeks 1–3)**  
Engage local learners and communities to identify gaps and define curriculum structure.

**Phase 2: Content Development and Localization (Weeks 4–8)**  
Develop lessons, translate into Hausa, and create beginner-friendly materials.

**Phase 3: Offline Toolkit Development (Weeks 6–9)**  
Create a fully offline learning package with datasets and installation guides.

**Phase 4: Pilot Implementation (Weeks 10–12)**  
Train 50–100 learners and 8–12 facilitators.

**Phase 5: Evaluation and Open-Source Release (Weeks 12–14)**  
Refine materials and publish them for global use.

---

### Sustainability

Sustainability will be achieved through a train-the-trainer model, enabling local facilitators to continue delivering training independently. All materials will be released as open-source to allow reuse and adaptation globally.

Partnerships with local communities such as R-Ladies Kano will support continued use and expansion. Future plans include scaling to other regions and developing advanced modules.
